how many system prompts do we have here? what are they?
The agent identified ~6 distinct injected instruction sources in the session context, categorizing them as core system prompts, advisor instructions, user config, memory, hooks, and tool manifests.
no, I mean in this current codebase
The agent found 10 distinct LLM system prompts across 4 pipelines (capture, inject, awareness, doctor), listing each with its file location and role.
which one does the pc archeologist importer use?
The archeologist reuses the same capture pipeline prompts (triage gate, EXTRACT, ROUTE, RECONCILE) with no archeologist-specific prompt, only differing in I/O routing.
so tell me during a normal archeologist session what do we send to the llm?

The agent explained the fixed sequence of LLM calls per session: 1 triage + 1 extract + 1 route + N reconcile calls, detailing the system and user content for each, plus periodic open-questions extraction.
how can I see a typical plain-text transcript? (an example)
The agent showed an example plain-text transcript and noted caveats: tool_use/tool_result/reasoning blocks were dropped for readability, and sidechain/meta entries are filtered in the real pipeline.

The agent detailed the complete filtering/truncation chain: text-only content filtering, sidechain/meta filtering (archeologist path), substance gate, dedup, and tail-keep truncation at 200k/250k chars, flagging head-truncation and tool-stripping as likely causes of wiki sparseness.
for truncation we should prefer to remove the in-between assistante messages, never user messages -- by in-between I mean assistant messages that are followed by assistant messages.

The agent implemented the new truncation strategy that drops "in-between" assistant turns (followed by another assistant turn) oldest-first instead of tail-slicing, preserving user turns and keeping all 84 tests passing.
